observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant
with the duly certified list of all taxpayers showing the name of taxpayers and the
amount due and collectible of each property owner at the beginning of the year as
basis in recording the RPT and SET Receivables.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
prepare a Journal Entry Voucher to record in the books for the General Fund the
PPEs procured in CY 2022 totaling ₱10,817,921.78; and
record, in subsequent procurement, assets funded from Trust Fund, to the
appropriate PPE and Inventory account and prepare the corresponding entry to
recognize the assets in the books of the General Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:

the Municipal Assessor to: (i) furnish the Municipal Accountant with copies of the Transfer
Certificates of Title to facilitate the recording of the titled lots in the LGU’s books of
accounts; and (ii) facilitate and supervise the proper proceeding for the land titling of the
identified 14 parcels of land, and the determination of ownership of the remaining 23
parcels of land in Table No. 5.

the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Assessor to set specific dates to reconcile
their records on the real estate properties owned by the Municipality.

We recommended that Municipal Accountant:
revert the unsupported and long-outstanding payables and make the necesary
adjustments in the books; and
record as Accounts Payable all unpaid services rendered and goods delivered as of
year-end.
We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer submit to the Municipal
Accountant obligated transactions with complete supporting documents as basis for
recording of Accounts Payable on or before the 31st of December every year.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
coordinate with and assist the Municipal Treasurer in the retrieval of the
unsubmitted DVs on paid checks totaling ₱2,444,575.43;
record the submitted DVs in the book of accounts, and thereafter submit immediately
the said documents to the Audit Team to avoid the issuance of Notice of Disallowance
on the paid transactions which were not documented pursuant to COA Circular No.
2009-006 dated September 15, 2009;
prepare the adjusting entry for the ₱29,815.00 deposits returned by the bank; and
review further previous accounting records on the bank account which was not
confirmed by the bank but has balances in the books and make the necessary
adjustments thereon.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer:
verify and validate the dormant accounts under the GF and SEF to determine the
possibility of closing the accounts and transferring the cash to other bank accounts
under the same fund.;
transfer the amounts in the savings accounts to current accounts and re-appropriate
for necessary projects that needs funding; and
return to the Bureau of Treasury the dormant balances pertaining to completed prior
years’ programs/projects, if the banks confirm the amounts. Revert to the General
Fund the unexpended amount which are determined to be the LGU’s counterpart.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
evaluate the existing balances in the Due to NGAs account and communicate with the
Source Agency (SA) to confirm whether LGU Palo have an existing liability to the
agency;
revert to the General Fund the unknown amounts and inform the Municipal Budget
Officer of such reversion after confirming with the Municipal Treasurer the
availability of the corresponding cash; and
prepare the Disbursement Voucher, in favor of the Bureau of Treasury, for the return
of excess balances from downloaded funds from the national government which are
confirmed.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare the certified list of
uncollected public market rentals every month, and submit the same to the Municipal
Accountant.

We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant record the receivables and the
related revenues in the books of the Municipality on the period it was earned based
on the list from the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant:
transfer to the LDRRMF Special Trust Fund the unexpended appropriation of CY
2022 totaling ₱11,754,078.80, after the enactment of an Ordinance approving its
utilization;
transfer, in the succeeding years, to the LDRRMF Special Trust Fund account, the
correct unexpended amount of the LDRRMF before the last working day of each year
to be used for disaster management activities of the LGU within five years from the
date of transfer; and
revert to the general fund the unutilized balance of LDRRMF which had lapsed the
5-year retention period totaling ₱102,499.21, to be programmed for other social
services of the Municipality identified by the local sanggunian.

We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and MDRRMO request for
the enactment of an Ordinance to make available the unexpended appropriation of
CY 2022 totaling ₱11,754,078.80, which were reverted to the surplus of the general
fund, prior to using the amount in CY 2023.

We recommended that the BAC follow strictly the rules and regulations set forth in
the 2016 RIRR of RA 9184, specifically on the following:
Invite three observers in all stages of the procurement activities;
Require the BAC-TWG to prepare Bid Evaluation Report;
Include in the notice to the bidder with the Lowest Calculated Bid the requirement
to submit their latest income and business tax returns, and other appropriate licenses
and permit required by law; and
Refrain from undertaking procurement proceedings without the minimum
composition of the BAC members.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor, henceforth, immediately issue an
Executive Order replacing or designating a new BAC member in case of resignation,
retirement, separation, transfer, reassignment and removal, in order to comply with
the necessary composition, set forth under Section 11.2.1 of the 2016 RIRR of RA
9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Treasurer,
conduct intensive tax campaign and information drive, aside from the required
posting of notices, to encourage more taxpayers to pay taxes; and follow-up
responses to the Notice of Delinquencies issued.
We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor exercise the taxing authority of
the LGU as mandated in the Local Government Code of 1991.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, the Municipal Budget Officer and the
Municipal Accountant discontinue the utilization of the 20% Development Fund for
administrative expenses to avoid the incurrence of irregular expenses.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor prepare, prior to procurement, the
justification on the engagement of individual consultant hired through Negotiated
Procurement-Highly Technical Consultants, together with the Terms of Reference.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
The BAC to undertake the negotiation with the individual consultant based on the
Terms of Reference prepared by the end-user; and to require the consultants to
submit the mandatory eligibility requirements before the Notice of Award is issued,
otherwise, the transactions shall be suspended in audit;
The BAC Secretariat to post in the PhilGEPS website the Notice of Award, Contract,
and Notice to Proceed for Negotiated Procurement-Highly Technical Consultants;
and
The Municipal Accountant process payments for consultancy services only when
supported with sufficient and complete documentation and payments are within the
validity period of the contracts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
impose the collection of rentals from delinquent market stallholders and business
owners, and impose Section 2D.08 of the Market Code on the revocation of lease for
stall holders who failed to pay rentals for three (3) months; and
sign a Contract of Lease Agreement with all existing market stall holders, and enforce
the terms and conditions of the contract in administering the collection of market stall
rentals.
We also recommended that the Market Supervisor and the Municipal Treasurer
maintain a list of all delinquent market stall holders and issue Demand Letters in
accordance with the Lease Contract Agreement Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant closely monitor the honoraria being
claimed by BAC, BAC Secretariat and TWG and ensure that the total honoraria are:
within the 25% basic monthly salary threshold;
based on successfully completed competitive bidding; and
not granted to personnel other that those mentioned in DBM Budget Circular 2004-
5A.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor, and the officials and employees
concerned, follow strictly the regulations pertaining to payments of procurement
honoraria to avoid disallowances in audit.

1.11 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. create an Inventory Committee composed of adequate number of
members that will conduct a complete annual physical count of LGU
properties;
b. issue a directive on the newly created Inventory Committee to:
i. prepare, for her approval, a Physical Inventory Plan containing the
assignments of Committee members, schedules/location of inventory-
taking and cut-off dates for the complete physical inventory of all
properties of the Municipality over a three-month period in CY 2022;
ii. implement the PIP and finish the physical inventory within the time
frame specified in CY 2022.
1.12 We also recommended that the newly created Inventory Committee:
a. consolidate the RPCPPE prepared by each office by grouping together
similar items, showing complete data on PPE Type and cost; and
b. submit the consolidated RPCPPE to the Municipal Accountant before
December 31, 2022 for reconciliation, copy furnished the Audit Team.
1.13 We further recommended that the General Service Office gather and
consolidate all of the Property Cards and then reconcile with the records of
the Accounting Office. Thereafter, we recommend that the GSO religiously
maintain complete and accurate Property Cards in order to promote
effective monitoring and controls over PPE.

2.7 We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant trace the date of
acquisition of the properties totaling ₱136,790,124.35 to determine whether
these are fully depreciated or not and compute/record the accumulated
depreciation if found otherwise, in order to present the correct carrying
values of the assets of the Municipality.

3.5 We recommended that the Municipal Engineer provide the OIC-Municipal
Accountant with the necessary data regarding the Road Networks of the
Municipality of Palo as basis of the latter in providing the complete
disclosure on the details of the Road Networks account in the Notes to the
Financial Statements.

4.9 We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant, in behalf of the
Municipal Mayor, file a request for write-off of the dormant accounts, after
all remedies have been exhausted, in accordance with COA Circular No.
2016-005 dated December 19, 2016, in order to reconcile and clear the books
of the LGU of dormant accounts.

5.6 We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant prepare the adjusting
entries for the ₱57,309.36 unidentified refunds and conduct account analysis
on the remaining ₱15,127.14 negative balance to determine whether these are
due for refund to the Accountable Officers or an error on the recording of
transactions.

6.10 We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer:
a) Obtain from DOH Regional Office and the Leyte Provincial Health
Office the actual information on the unit cost and quantities of the
vaccines received by the LGU in relation to its vaccination program;
b) Provide the Municipal Accountant with the complete documents from
the DOH and LPHO relative to the receipt of the vaccines, including
the extracted data from the QVAC System on the doses administered
at the end of each month.

6.11 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant record using the
perpetual inventory system, the vaccines received by the LGU and the doses
dispensed, using as reference the report/documents submitted by the MHO.

7.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue a directive, based on the
report of the Municipal Accountant, to all accountable officers/employees
with unliquidated cash advances as of December 31, 2021, requiring them to
submit their Liquidation Reports within 30 days from receipt of such
directive, and providing therein the condition to withhold any money due
them in case of failure to comply.

7.9 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant desist from processing
the payment of additional cash advances to officers and employees with
unliquidated previous cash advances.

8.10 We recommended that the BAC utilize framework agreements for the
procurement of goods and services which by their nature, may repeatedly be
required, at quantities or times of need, that cannot be accurately pre-
determined in order to promote efficiency in the procurement process.

8.11 We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor provide assistance to local
suppliers on their compliance with the registration requirements in
PhilGEPS and RA 9184 to afford them better opportunities in future
contracts with the LGU to compete for bigger procurement contracts.

9.9 We recommended that the BAC update the indicative APP of the
Municipality to include the consolidated requisitions of similar supplies,
materials & equipment to avoid splitting on requisitions and contracts.

9.10 We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor approve only those
procurements which are in accordance with the updated APP, in strict
observation of the pertinent provisions of RA 9184 and its applicable IRR on
the prohibition against splitting of contracts in procurement transactions, in
order to prevent incurrence of audit disallowances.

10.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor sign an individual MOA with the
Punong Barangay of each recipient barangay, prior to the release of future fund
transfers, in order to ensure that the barangay officials will comply with the
terms and conditions of the LGU and funds will actually be used for the
intended purpose and within the allotted timeframe.

11.10 We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Budget Officer discontinue the practice of paying prior year’s expenditures
from current year appropriations to avoid unauthorized and irregular
expenditures.

12.19 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive implement the programs,
projects and activities in the LDRRMO Work and Financial Plan in order to
utilize the LDRRMF and fully equip the Municipality in the management of
risks and effects of disasters.

12.20 We also recommended that the Municipal DRRM Officer:
d) Prepare the appropriate document for the LDRRMF Investment Plan
in accordance with the form prescribed by COA Circular 2012-002
dated September 12, 2012;
e) Specify the funding of each PPAs under each Thematic Area; and
f) Provide specific periods of implementation of each PPAs.

1.11. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive create an Inventory
Committee to conduct a physical count of all PPE and to prepare a report
thereon, using RPCPPE form, as well as designate a General Services
Officer (GSO) for proper custody, accountability and record management
of all PPE of the Municipality. Likewise, require the following offices to:
a) Accounting Office -
1. Coordinate with the Inventory Committee for the reconciliation
of accounting records on PPE against physical count;
2. Draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to set-up an allowance for
depreciation for the accounts Road Network, Flood Control
System, Water System, School Buildings, Hospitals and Health
Centers, Slaughterhouses, Military, Police, Security Equipment
and Agricultural and Forest Equipment;
3. Coordinate with GSO and Municipal Agriculture Office to locate
records and details of work/zoo as to its existence, distribution or
death to determine appropriate accounting procedures/entries to
be undertaken;
4. Provide disclosure on the Road Network System of the
Municipality in the Notes to Financial Statements; and
5. Revise the subsidiary ledgers maintained for Road Networks into
per Road and its components, rather than per project or activity;
b) General Services Office –
To prepare the following reports for the Road Network System:
1. Report on the Local Road Network following the format provided
in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2015-008;
2. Report on the Physical Count on Road Network System,
prescribed in Anne C of the COA Circular; and
3. Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its
components, pursuant to Annex E of the same COA Circular.

2.8 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to
analyze the reciprocal accounts to determine the causes of the discrepancies
and adjust the books accordingly; henceforth, to periodically reconcile the
reciprocal accounts.

3.8 We recommended that the:
a) Municipal Agriculturist prepare and submit the Inventory of the
Biological Asset every year-end and furnish the Municipal
Accountant with documents relative to addition, distribution, and
death of the breeding stocks pursuant to Section 4.5 of COA
Circular No. 2016-004; and
b) Municipal Accountant prepares the Journal Entry Voucher to
adjust the account balance depending on the report provided by
the Municipal Agriculturist and in accordance with the
recognition and measurement principle provided in IPSAS 27.

4.15 We recommended that the Municipality should:
a) Maximize the utilization of appropriated funds in implementing
the various programs for risk reduction and management in
order to enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities;
b) Ensure the timely preparation and submission of the monthly
Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRM Fund and the
Registry of Appropriation, Allotments and Obligations (RAAO)
in compliance with Sections 5.1.4 and 5.1.5 of COA Circular No.
2012-002, dated September 12, 2012; and
c) Prepare annually, the LDRRM Fund Investment Plan and
include the prior years’ unutilized LDRRM Fund in the
preparation of said plan as required in Section 5.1.2 of the same
COA Circular.

5.10 We recommended that the Municipal Engineering Office be required
to:
a) Carry out sufficient detailed engineering and ensure that RROW
issues are resolved prior to the procurement of infrastructure
projects;
b) Approve extension and variation orders in strict compliance with
Annex E of the IRR of RA 9184; and
c) Strictly monitor the implementation and timely completion of the
infradtructure projects.

6.7 We recommended that the Municipal Development Council ensure
that sufficient or specific details of the programs, projects and activities to
be funded out of the Development Fund be indicated in the Annual
Investment Plan, pursuant to Section 454(d) of the IRR of RA 7160 and
Section 2 of EO 189, s. 1999.

7.8 We recommended that the Municipality stop granting of cash
advances for pre-determined expenditures where direct payment to
supplier/creditor is possible, and that the Municipal Accountant refrain
from accepting partial liquidation. It is also recommended that no cash
advances shall be granted for the regular and special disbursing officer
unless the previous cash advance has been fully settled in compliance to
COA Circular No. 97-002.

8.9 We recommended that:
a) The RCCs concerned and the Municipal Treasurer be required to
produce the unaccounted AFs and relieved them from their post
until such time that said AFs are fully accounted. Henceforth,
require RCCs to render their report of accountability for AFs in
the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAFs) at
least once a month and the Municipal Treasurer to
summarize/consolidate these reports in the Consolidated Report
of Accountability for Accountable Forms (CRAAFs), and submit
the consolidated report accompanied by the individual reports to
the Audit Team not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month.
Moreover, stop the practice of assigning Job Order personnel as
collectors;
b) The Disbursing Officer be adequately bonded in accordance with
Annex C of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009;
c) The accountable officers be required to maintain and update
regularly the prescribed Cash book. Require also the Municipal
Treasurer to to strictly comply with the deposit of collections
daily and intact as provided for in Section 69 (1) of P.D. 1445 and
Section 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382.
d) The issuance of AFs be supported with Requisition Issue Slip for
recording and monitoring purposes pursuant to Section 59.P of
the DOF-BLGF Local Treasury Operations Manual.

9.7 We recommended that management maximize the utilization of the
GAD funds through the implementation of GAD related programs and
projects in order to attain the objective for which the funds were provided.

We recommended that henceforth, the Municipal Accountant
recognize in the books all completed projects as PPE, regardless of fund
source and billing received, by basing its cost from the Contract Agreement.

We recommended that the Municipality demand from the concerned
barangays the immediate liquidation of fund transfers and require them to
submit the Report of Checks Issued (RCI) and Report of Disbursements
(ROD) to facilitate the recording of its liquidation.

We recommended that management thru Sangguniang Bayan update
its Local Revenue Code pursuant to Section 186 and 191 of RA 7160, in
order to increase the financial capability of the LGU in rendering basic
mandated services. Likewise, they should formulate a separate Ordinance
for the collection of rental payment from private or non-official use of the
Municipal Function Hall.

We recommended that management give priority in providing
solution to the problem on road right-of–way to expedite the
implementation of the local access road to Brgy. San Jose, Palo, Leyte up to
end of CY 2019, otherwise, return immediately the whole amount to the
National Treasury pursuant to Section 6.4.5.1 of DILG Memorandum
Circular No. 2018-61.

We recommended that the MDRRM Focal Person of LGU-Palo,
Leyte, consider the other equally important projects and activities under
disaster preparedness such as, the conduct of simulation exercise to test
plans and skills; development of education, information and communication
campaign; etc., in the appropriation of fund pursuant to Section 5.2 of
NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 and
maximize its utilization in order for the constituents to develop and
strengthen their preparedness and capabilities in times of disaster.

We recommended that the LGU conduct gender analysis through the
administration of HGDG to GAD attributed projects pursuant to Section
C.4 of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No.
2013-01, to serve as basis on the gender responsiveness of the program and
to determine the appropriate percentage/amount to be accredited to GAD
Budget.
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should compel the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries, if any.
observations.managementAction: Accountant verify the transactions in the accounts which brought about the abnormal balances and could not verify the current personnel service, hence no adjusting entries were prepared.
observations.reason: The negative balance existed way back in 1990’s before any one from the Organization and Accounting personnel was in service, and failed to prepare any adjusting entries, if any.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management immediate cause the return of the unused financial assistance to NHA in accordance with the provisions of the MOA.
observations.managementAction: Returned to NHA the amount after coordination.
observations.reason: The original purpose can no longer be realized.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor communicate with concerned officials of the Organization and require the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash advances pursuant to COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal AAR requested the agency to submit the request to write-off the account. The agency has not yet submitted such request to COA.
observations.reason: The amount of P100,000.00 was granted as a loan to a cooperative in which collection is impossible since the said cooperative was not anymore operational. No request to write-off the account has been submitted by the agency to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer reconcile her cashbook balances every end of the month or as the need arises with the subsidiary accounting records as required in Section 63 (F) of the Local Treasury Operations Manual.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare appropriate adjusting journal entries to correct the understatement of the Due from LGU and overstatement of Donation accounts and that subsequent similar transactions be recorded in the proper account pursuant to Section 111 and 121 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 for fair presentation in the financial statements
We further recommend the submission of the liquidation report by the
concerned thirteen barangays as required in the Memorandum of Agreement and for validation as to the propriety of the disbursements made.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant be required to submit immediately the disbursement vouchers enumerated in Annex F, together with its supporting documents. Henceforth, strictly observe the timely submission of accounts pursuant to Section 347 RA 7160 and Section 7.2.1 of COA Circular 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommend for strict observance of the guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund as provided for DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-01 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommend that the municipality maximize utilization of appropriated funds in implementing the various programs for risk reduction and management in order to enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities. Likewise, require the MDDRM focal person to prepare and submit the LDRRMF Investment Plan and Report on Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF and the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to transfer to the Special Trust Fund the unexpended balances of LDRRMF from CY 2013 to CY 2017 pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommend that management appropriate in the LGU’s Annual Budget in the ensuing year, 1% of its IRA for the implementation of LCPC-identified programs, projects and activities for child protection and welfare and prevention of juvenile delinquency as required by RA 9344 and reiterated in DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.
We also recommend that the created LCPC prepare the following documents for
inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan and Annual Budget: a.) LCPC Annual Work and
Financial Plan prioritized according to needs for integration in the local development plan;
b.) Local Development Plan for Children (LDPC), and; c.) Local Investment Plan for
Children (LCC).

We recommend that management strictly adhere with the required payment of checks directly to supplier except for small value items that could be paid out of petty cash fund pursuant to the General Principles in COA Circular 97-002. Further, stop the practice of reimbursement, rather strictly observe the procurement law to promote transparency in all transactions and in compliance to Section 2 of PD 1445 for an effective, economical and efficient government operation.

We recommend that for an effective information and education campaign, it
should be collaborated with the Barangay officials concerned. We further recommend that the Municipality require its hired garbage collector to provide a separate collection schedules and or separate trucks or haulers for specific types of wastes, otherwise the truck must have a suitable compartment for storing sorted waste in compliance with Section 22 and 24 of RA 9003 and in the Municipality’s approved 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan.

We recommend that the Local School Board be directed to re-evaluate its School
Board Budget to include ECCD Program and strictly comply with RA 7160 and
DepED, DBM, DILG Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2017. Likewise, require the Local
School Board, Municipal Accountant, and Municipal Budget Officer to use the
prescribe forms for SEF Budget Preparation and SEF Budget Accountability.

We recommend that management enforce the contractor to do corrective repair
otherwise management must undertake the necessary repair on tile works and
reimbursed all expenses to the contractor pursuant to Sections 62.2.3.1 and 62.2.4
of the IRR of RA 9184.

We recommend that the GAD Focal Point System meet regularly in order to
update the GAD Fund utilization, monitor its implementation and provide assistance to
achieve a hundred percent implementation of all PPAs identified in the GAD Plan and
Budget in compliance to Section 36 of R.A. No. 9710 and Section 2.3 of the Joint
Circular No. 2012-01 of the PCW, NEDA, and DBM and other rules and regulations on
GAD.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management: a. account in detail the official receipts issued to all revenue collectors since the time they assumed the responsibilities in handling cash collections and require the immediate remittance of collections, if any; b. stop the issuance of additional accountable forms to these revenue collectors listed in Annex B.
observations.managementAction: Treasurer: a. account in detail the official receipts issued to all revenue collectors; b. no additional accountable forms has been issued to these revenue collectors

observations.recommendation: We recommend that immediate liquidation be made for the balance of the cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Liquidation Report of the CA balance of P102,255.32 totaling only.
observations.reason:
all outstanding cash advances totaling
P292,390.32 remain
unliquidated

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the unutilized balance of the PDAF be refunded/return the balance of the PDAF to the Bureau of the Treasury in the total amount of P2,949,360.31.
observations.managementAction: The amount of P2,949,360.31 has been returned/depo sited to the account of the National Treasury thru LBP Check No. 3438596 dated March 2, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer coordinate with the contractor to exert earnest effort to fast-track implementation of the project.
observations.managementAction:
The project required, but was temporarily stop the thresh-out as it interfere with the on-going construction of the public market.
The Municipal Engineer coordinate with the contractor to fast-track the implementation of the project.
observations.reason: Started the project with the contractor

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management comply with the provisions of the MOA as follows: a. Transfer the balance of P2,299,537.01 to separate COMBO/Saving account of the LGU for the funds on the form of under its Program; and b. Provide the LGU’s counterpart to the project in the total amount of P550,642.00.
observations.managementAction: On the other hand, we recommend that the Municipal Treasurer provide the balance of the funds released by the DOLE under its Kabuhayan Program; The LGU’s counterpart for the project amounting P550,642.00 was in the form of personnel services, building and MOOE.
observations.reason: a. Transfer the balance of P2,299,537.01 or to separate COMBO/Saving Account of the LGU for the funds released by the DOLE under its Kabuhayan Program; and b. Provide the LGU’s counterpart to the project in the total amount of P550,642.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the unutilized amount of P1,510,000.00 be returned to DSWD in accordance with the provisions of the MOA.
observations.managementAction: Returned to DSWD the amount of P1,510,000.00 thru LBP check no. 3252742 dated May 24, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management: ascertain the total accountability of the deceased Municipal Treasurer, and the former OIC-Municipal Treasurer, and to account for all outgoing Municipal Treasurer forms issued and to make an invoice by the outgoing Municipal Treasurer and make formal turnover to the current Municipal Treasurer.
observations.managementAction: A task force headed by the Assistant Treasurer was created to account for the deceased OIC and the outgoing accountable forms issued and account for the outgoing Municipal Treasurer forms issued for formal turnover to the current Municipal Treasurer.
observations.reason: For CY 2013 ORs and on-going with CY 2014 & 2015.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer be required to submit promptly the individual and Consolidated Financial Statements for the year ended December 31, 2016, and to strictly comply with the provisions of the Accounting Systems Manual for Barangays.
observations.managementAction: Reports were submitted on March 2016. Municipal Budget Officer and Accountant be required to submit promptly the individual and consolidated reports/information in compliance with COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: require the Accountant to prepare the monthly Report on the Receipt and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (DRRM) Fund and submit to the COA Auditor thru the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) every 15th day of the month; require the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare and maintain a separate Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (RAAO) for Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) Quick Response Fund, Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses and Capital Outlay; and submit immediately the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP).
observations.managementAction:
The LGU for coming up with the 100% completion of the subprojects under RAY Project. On the other hand, we recommend that the Municipal Treasurer prepare the monthly Report of project Disbursements in compliance with Section 3e, Article III of the MOA on the RAY Project.
Management has been issuing monthly report to DILG and DBM. An AOM on the non-submission was issued through AOM No. 2018-006(2017), dated April 17, 2018.
observations.reason:
RAAF was kept and disclosure on said fund was made in the Notes to Financial Statements
CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor compel the Municipal Treasurer prepare the monthly report of project disbursements in compliance with Section 3e, Article III of the MOA.
observations.managementAction: The LGU for coming up with the 100% completion of the subprojects under RAY Project. On the other hand, we recommend that the Municipal Treasurer prepare the monthly Report of project Disbursements in compliance with Section 3e, Article III of the MOA on the RAY Project.
observations.reason: On the other hand, one project which is the rehabilitation of the Brgy. Hall of Brgy. Libertad was found during inspection to be not fit for occupancy due to defective flooring. AOM No. 2018-0012(2017) dated April 30, 2018 was issued for said project.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management: a. immediate liquidation of all outstanding cash advances; b. stop the practice of granting cash advances to employees with previous unliquidated cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Immediate liquidation of all outstanding cash advances; stop the practice of granting cash advances to employees with previous unliquidated cash advances.
observations.reason: Some of the cash advances are still not yet fully liquidated. Ongoing thru payroll deductions.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management compose its reports/information and submit the required reports in compliance with COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.
observations.managementAction: Reports were submitted. The monthly and project reports for CY 2017 were prepared and submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local School Board: a. Meet regularly at least once a month in accordance with the provision of Section 100 of RA No. 7160; b. Prepare early, in the succeeding years, the annual SEF budget with complete information as to the programs, projects and activities for the ensuing fiscal year; and c. Ensure the full and proper utilization of the SEF budget in accordance with the priority expenses mandated by laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor has required the Local School Board to meet regularly and prepare the required SEF reports. On the other hand, the SEF budget for CY 2017 was prepared and approved.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the local School Board avoid disbursements which are not among the priority expenses intended for the SEF.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation in CY 2017. No payment were charged against SEF for non-priority items.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management: require the accountable officers to liquidate/refund immediately their cash advances; and require the other three (3) accountable officers to refund immediately their cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Four (4) of the accountable custodians are active and already made their liquidation. One custodian, Frank Villablanca, Private Secretary, resigned without claiming his terminal benefits.
observations.reason: One custodian, Frank Villablanca, who is said to be connected with LGU Pastrana now.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management: 1. make representations with the DBP to reverse the bank service charges; 2. transfer dormant bank accounts to an account which is income generating, if it is not contrary to the MOA for the trust fund accounts; and 3. for those Trust Fund bank accounts which have been dormant for over two (2) years for the reason of non-utilization, revert back to the Bureau of the Treasury the unutilized balance and, in effect, close the bank account.
observations.managementAction: Representation with DBP has been made. DBP promised to revert said charges this CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management: a. designate a supply officer; b. require the Municipal Accountant to record supplies using the perpetual inventory system.
observations.managementAction: Supply Officer has been designated, and procurement of supplies is no longer recorded directly as expense.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the concerned PEI grant of P1,650,000.00 to refund the excess amount received for CY 2014.
observations.managementAction: Management request the current audit team to re-evaluate said disbursements with the guidelines for PEI in CY 2014.
observations.reason: Management contends that their grant of PEI in CY 2014 was not contrary to the guidelines set.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the concerned accountable officers to adhere to the provisions of Section 89 of P. D. No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Liquidated on December 2014 per JEV No.100-2014-12-50087 for P833,869.15 the balance of P30,130.85 was deducted in L. Fernandez CNA & PEI benefits per JEV # 100-2015-07-30884.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to strictly adhere to the provisions of Section V.B of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014.
observations.managementAction: Distribution list submitted covers CY 2013 only.
observations.reason: Current audit team is asking for proof of submission.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management organize the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board and formulate the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Solid Waste Board has been organized and the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan formulated and approved by EMB in CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management provide the Treasury Office with an enclosed office space only accessible by legitimate purpose and a durable safe/vault where the accountable officers can safely keep their cash and accountabilities.
observations.managementAction: Funds in custody of accountable officers are now provided with an enclosed office space and a durable safe/vault where the accountable officers can safely keep their cash and accountabilities.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare monthly RAAF.
observations.managementAction: RAAF has been prepared and submitted to COA in March 2017 with the issuance of check No. 3438596 in favor of the Bu. Of Treasury for P1,458,828.47.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management immediately refund/return the PDAF to the Bureau of the Treasury the total amount of P1,458,828.47.
observations.managementAction: Refund/returned to the Bu. Of Treasury for P1,458,828.47.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management should: 1. Require the Accountant to prepare the monthly Report on the Receipt and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (DRRM) Fund and submit to the COA Auditor thru the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) every 15th day of the month. 2. Require the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare and maintain a separate Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (RAAO) for Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) Quick Response Fund, Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses and Capital Outlay. 3. Submit immediately the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP).
observations.managementAction: Management has been submitting required monthly status report to DILG and DBM. And issued AOM No. 2018-006(2017) dated April 17, 2018 for non-submission. A separate registry for 2013 LDRRMF has been maintained and submitted to COA.
observations.reason: An AOM No. 2018-006 (2017) was issued on this matter and incorporated in CY 2017 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management: require officials concerned to strictly adhere to Section 4, paragraph 6 of PD 1445 on the submission of complete documents to support payment for claims; require the CIPLC to update and provide a complete information about the account as required under the above rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor should compel the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjusting entries to correct the over and under statements of the accounts affected in compliance with paragraphs 1 and 2 of Sections 111 and 112 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 2004-008.
observations.reason: Current audit team is asking for proof of submission.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management: compel the Municipal Accountant to prepare necessary adjusting entries to correct the over and under statements of the accounts affected; and require the Municipal Treasurer to hold the deductions until full payment of the claim of the contractor as indicated in his letter request to hold the deductions.
observations.managementAction: The recorded accounts payable are now reliable and the mandatory deductions were considered in the computation and have been withheld. The deductions will be made upon full payment of the contract.
observations.reason: The agency has not yet fully paid the contractor due to pending release of funds by DAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management strictly follow the Guidelines in the implementation of the Feeding Program.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor has ordered strict compliance to the DSWD guidelines in the implementation of the feeding program and the support documents are being compiled as required.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor and concerned officials strictly adhere to the guidelines in the utilization of the 20% development fund outlined in DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012 was revised and the Implementing Guidelines in the utilization of 20% Development Fund was strictly followed and incorporated in CY 2017 AAR.
observations.reason: An AOM No. 2018-009 (2017) was issued on this matter and incorporated in CY 2017 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require officials concerned to deduct VAT and EWT from every progress billing on payments.
observations.managementAction: EWT and VAT were deducted from every progress billing pursuant to BIR Revenue Regulations No. 2-98 dated April 17, 1998, Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 5-2006.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Engineer conduct a physical inventory
of the public infrastructures to be used as basis in the recognition of Road
Network account in the books of accounts by the Municipal Accountant in the
books by the Municipal Accountant in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-
008.

We recommend that management assign a committee to conduct and submit
the actual physical inventory of its property, plant and equipment.
We did not issue NS or ND for this audit observation because it pertains to a
procedural lapse.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant closely monitor the
submission by the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate early submission of
transaction documents and financial reports and keep up the good
performance of submitting on time the year-end financial statements.
This is without prejudice of the issuance of NS/ND upon post-audit of
transaction documents which were submitted late.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer:
a. account in detail the official receipts issued to all collectors since the
time these revenue collectors assumed the handling cash collections and
require the immediate remittance of collections, if any;
b. stop the issuance of additional accountable forms to these revenue
collectors listed in Annex B.

We recommend that immediate liquidation be made for the balance of the
cash advances totaling P292,390.32.
This is without prejudice to the issuance of NS/ND, if warranted.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer be
required to immediately refund/return the balance of the PDAF to the Bureau of
the Treasury in the total amount of P2,949,360.31.

We recommend that the Municipal Engineer coordinate with the
contractor to thresh-out problems and exert earnest effort to fast-track the
implementation of the project.
This is without prejudice to the issuance of NS/ND where warranted.

We recommend that Management comply with the provisions of the MOA
as follows:
a. Transfer the balance of P2,299,537.01 to separate combo/Savings
Account of the LGU for the funds released by the DOLE under its
Livelihood (Kabuhayan) Program; and
b. Provide the LGU’s counterpart to the project in the total amount of
P550,642.00.

We recommend that the unutilized balance of the ESA amounting to
P1,510,000.00 be returned to DSWD in compliance with Paragraph II (f), Section
3 of the MOA to correct the overstatement of Cash in Bank-Local Currency,
Current Account and Due to NGAs.

We recommend that Management ascertain the total accountability of the
deceased OIC Municipal Treasurer, and the outgoing Municipal Treasurer make
formal invoice and receipt of accountabilities for formal turnover to the incumbent
Municipal Treasurer.
We did not issue NS/ND on this audit observation because it pertains to a
procedural lapse.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget
Officer be required to submit promptly the individual and Consolidated Financial
Statements for the year ended December 31, 2016, and Consolidated Status of
Appropriations, Commitments and Balances (CSACB) for CYs 2010-2016, and to
strictly comply with the provisions of the Accounting Systems Manual for
Barangays.
This audit observation does not warrant the issuance of Notice of Suspension
(NS)/Notice of Disallowance (ND).

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be required to keep
subsidiary ledgers, make proper disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements
and regularly submit the required monthly reports in strict compliance with COA
Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
We did not issue NS/ND because this audit observation pertains to procedural
deficiency.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant verify the transactions in the
accounts which brought about the abnormal balances and prepare the necessary
adjusting journal entries, if any.
We did not issue NS/ND on this audit observation because it pertains to
accounting deficiency.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer prepare the monthly Report of
Disbursements in compliance with Section 3e, Article III of the MOA.
